initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. Cost to produce was 40 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE countered with 47 ZUP. This is above my cost of 40 ZUP, so I would make a 7 ZUP profit. However, my initial offer was 50 ZUP. Since I have 2 proposals left (this is my 2nd), I should try to get closer to my target of 50 ZUP before accepting. A compromise of 48 or 49 ZUP seems reasonable. I will propose 49 ZUP to see if they can stretch their budget one last time before I consider accepting their 47 ZUP or rejecting. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 49 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate your offer of 47 ZUP. While it is a profit for me, my initial asking price was 50 ZUP. To meet you halfway and close the deal quickly, I can lower my price to 49 ZUP. This is just 1 ZUP away from your offer and 1 ZUP below my original price. Please let me know if you can meet me at 49 ZUP. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags are present in the correct order. The trade string is parseable.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. The proposed price of 49 ZUP yields a 9 ZUP profit, which is better than the opponent's 47 ZUP (7 ZUP profit) and very close to the initial 50 ZUP target. It maximizes utility while remaining a small concession from the original ask.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent expressed interest and offered 47 ZUP. Asking for 49 ZUP (only 2 ZUP higher than their offer) is a reasonable "final" counter that respects their budget constraints while testing their upper limit. It is highly likely to be accepted or lead to an acceptance of the 47 ZUP offer if this is rejected.
4. Consistency: OK. The reasoning explicitly states the intent to propose 49 ZUP to meet halfway, and the message accurately reflects this logic without contradiction.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the 2nd proposal (limit is 3), so proposing is allowed. The format and tags strictly follow the rules.
</critique>
